gaps.title: Signal Extraction Race Condition
gaps.breaks: Concurrent signal extraction requests for same ticket cause incorrect signal values
gaps.scenario: Two support agents process the same high-value ticket simultaneously, causing the monetary value signal to be calculated incorrectly as $500 instead of $5,000
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates 10 concurrent requests to the signal extraction endpoint for the same ticket ID with text containing a monetary value. Verify that only one request processes the signal extraction while others use cached value, and that all requests return the correct monetary value signal.

gaps.title: Tenant Isolation in Signal Cache
gaps.breaks: Signals from one tenant leak to another due to cache key collision
gaps.scenario: Tenant A with "VIP customer" text and Tenant B with "regular customer" text get each other's customer tier signals due to insufficient tenant isolation in Redis cache keys
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates two tickets from different tenants with identical text but different expected customer tier signals. Verify that the signal extraction system includes tenant_id in Redis cache keys to prevent signal leakage between tenants.

gaps.title: Ambiguous Intent Classification
gaps.breaks: Multi-label classifier fails on ambiguous intent combinations
gaps.scenario: Ticket text "I want my money back for this broken feature that's not working" confuses the classifier, outputting both refund and technical intents with low confidence, causing incorrect routing
gaps.prompt: Write a test that sends 50 ambiguous ticket texts with overlapping intent categories (refund/technical, billing/complaint, etc.). Verify the classifier handles these edge cases gracefully and provides meaningful confidence scores for each intent.

gaps.title: Intent-Technique Mapping Failure
gaps.breaks: Incorrect mapping of secondary intents to AI techniques
gaps.scenario: A ticket with primary intent "refund" and secondary intent "complaint" gets mapped only to Self-Consistency technique instead of the combination of Self-Consistency + UoT+Reflexion
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies the intent×technique mapping correctly handles all primary/secondary intent combinations. Include test cases for each of the 6 intents with all possible secondary intents to ensure proper technique selection.

gaps.title: CLARA Pipeline Silent Failure
gaps.breaks: CLARA quality gate passes invalid responses due to silent stage failures
gaps.scenario: The Brand stage fails but doesn't trigger regeneration or human review, allowing a response with incorrect brand voice to proceed to the customer
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates failures in each of the 5 CLARA pipeline stages. Verify that any stage failure triggers the appropriate fallback behavior (regeneration or human review) and that the system logs the failure reason.

gaps.title: Signal Cache Staleness
gaps.breaks: Using stale signals after TTL expiration but before cache refresh
gaps.scenario: A signal's TTL expires while the system is processing, causing the classification to use outdated signal values that don't reflect the current ticket state
gaps.prompt: Write a test that artificially reduces the signal cache TTL to 1 second, then sends classification requests at 0.5-second intervals after TTL expiration. Verify that the system either uses the latest signals or handles the cache miss gracefully without using stale data.

gaps.title: Variant Weight Inconsistency
gaps.breaks: Different signal variant weights produce identical classification results
gaps.scenario: Two different signal variants with significantly different weights produce the same intent classification, indicating the weights are not being applied correctly
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates two signal variants with extreme weight differences (e.g., monetary weight 0.1 vs 0.9) for the same ticket text. Verify that the classification results differ significantly based on the variant weights applied.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 7

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Signal Extraction Race Condition
What breaks: Concurrent signal extraction requests for same ticket cause incorrect signal values
Real scenario: Two support agents process the same high-value ticket simultaneously, causing the monetary value signal to be calculated incorrectly as $500 instead of $5,000
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates 10 concurrent requests to the signal extraction endpoint for the same ticket ID with text containing a monetary value. Verify that only one request processes the signal extraction while others use cached value, and that all requests return the correct monetary value signal.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Tenant Isolation in Signal Cache
What breaks: Signals from one tenant leak to another due to cache key collision
Real scenario: Tenant A with "VIP customer" text and Tenant B with "regular customer" text get each other's customer tier signals due to insufficient tenant isolation in Redis cache keys
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates two tickets from different tenants with identical text but different expected customer tier signals. Verify that the signal extraction system includes tenant_id in Redis cache keys to prevent signal leakage between tenants.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Ambiguous Intent Classification
What breaks: Multi-label classifier fails on ambiguous intent combinations
Real scenario: Ticket text "I want my money back for this broken feature that's not working" confuses the classifier, outputting both refund and technical intents with low confidence, causing incorrect routing
AI agent prompt: Write a test that sends 50 ambiguous ticket texts with overlapping intent categories (refund/technical, billing/complaint, etc.). Verify the classifier handles these edge cases gracefully and provides meaningful confidence scores for each intent.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Intent-Technique Mapping Failure
What breaks: Incorrect mapping of secondary intents to AI techniques
Real scenario: A ticket with primary intent "refund" and secondary intent "complaint" gets mapped only to Self-Consistency technique instead of the combination of Self-Consistency + UoT+Reflexion
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies the intent×technique mapping correctly handles all primary/secondary intent combinations. Include test cases for each of the 6 intents with all possible secondary intents to ensure proper technique selection.

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: CLARA Pipeline Silent Failure
What breaks: CLARA quality gate passes invalid responses due to silent stage failures
Real scenario: The Brand stage fails but doesn't trigger regeneration or human review, allowing a response with incorrect brand voice to proceed to the customer
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates failures in each of the 5 CLARA pipeline stages. Verify that any stage failure triggers the appropriate fallback behavior (regeneration or human review) and that the system logs the failure reason.

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Signal Cache Staleness
What breaks: Using stale signals after TTL expiration but before cache refresh
Real scenario: A signal's TTL expires while the system is processing, causing the classification to use outdated signal values that don't reflect the current ticket state
AI agent prompt: Write a test that artificially reduces the signal cache TTL to 1 second, then sends classification requests at 0.5-second intervals after TTL expiration. Verify that the system either uses the latest signals or handles the cache miss gracefully without using stale data.

GAP 7
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Variant Weight Inconsistency
What breaks: Different signal variant weights produce identical classification results
Real scenario: Two different signal variants with significantly different weights produce the same intent classification, indicating the weights are not being applied correctly
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates two signal variants with extreme weight differences (e.g., monetary weight 0.1 vs 0.9) for the same ticket text. Verify that the classification results differ significantly based on the variant weights applied.
